content: Active agentic retrieval search agents can be re-framed through the biological definition of agents: just as pathogenic biological agents actively navigate host environments, target specific cell receptors, and adapt their behavior to evade host defenses to locate their desired 'resource', active search agents actively navigate unstructured information spaces, adapt their search queries to avoid irrelevant result traps, and target specific information receptors (user intent) to locate the exact requested knowledge. This framing leads to a novel hypothesis: biological virulence factors that improve a pathogen's ability to locate target hosts can inspire new search heuristics that improve retrieval accuracy for agentic search systems.
source_topics: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
trigger_topic: Active Agentic Retrieval with Search Agents
